comment_text.sn1.39:1.3: Pajjuna is Parjanya, the Vedic god of rain, especially the thunderstorm. His “lively drops” impregnate the earth, begetting plants (Rig Veda 5.83). While I cannot find a reference elsewhere to his daughter Kokanadā, it seems fitting that the daughter of the rain and the earth is the “Red Lotus”.
comment_text.sn1.39:2.2: Bodhi suggests taking the singular sattassa as a metrically shortened sattānaṁ (“beings”). However, there is a plural form sattāna that is metrically identical with sattassa. Perhaps Kokanadā is using satta here in the sense of “creation”.
comment_text.sn1.39:4.3: The commentaries identify this  roruva (“Hell of Screams”) with the “Smoky Roruva”, while the “Great Hell of Screams”, mentioned as the destiny of a corrupt financier in SN 3.20, is the “Flaming Roruva”, said to be the same as Avīci. These are also known in the Sanskrit tradition, eg. Manu 4.88, and described in detail at eg. Mārkaṇḍeya Purāṇa 10, 12. There, the Ruarava of glowing coals is the destiny of the false witness and the liar, while Mahāraurava is scorching copper and ravenous beasts.
comment_text.sn1.39:4.4: Torment in the Mahāraurava lasts 100,000,000 years, according to the Mārkaṇḍeya Purāṇa.

root_text.sn1.39:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.
root_text.sn1.39:1.3: Atha kho kokanadā pajjunnassa dhītā abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇā kevalakappaṁ mahāvanaṁ obhāsetvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi.
root_text.sn1.39:1.4: Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho sā devatā kokanadā pajjunnassa dhītā bhagavato santike imā gāthāyo abhāsi:

translation_text.sn1.39:0.2: 4. The Host of a Hundred Felicities
translation_text.sn1.39:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, at the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof.
translation_text.sn1.39:1.3: Then, late at night, the glorious Kokanadā, Pajjunna’s daughter, lighting up the entire Great Wood, went up to the Buddha, bowed, stood to one side,
translation_text.sn1.39:1.4: and recited these verses in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.39:2.2: is the Buddha, best of creation.
translation_text.sn1.39:2.3: Kokanadā am I who worships him,
translation_text.sn1.39:3.2: the teaching realized by the Clear-eyed One.
translation_text.sn1.39:3.3: But now I know it as a witness
translation_text.sn1.39:3.4: while the sage, the Holy One teaches.
translation_text.sn1.39:4.1: There are simpletons who go about
translation_text.sn1.39:4.2: denouncing the teaching of the noble ones.
translation_text.sn1.39:4.3: They fall into the terrible Hell of Screams
translation_text.sn1.39:5.1: There are those who have found  <j>acceptance and peace
translation_text.sn1.39:5.2: in the teaching of the noble ones.
translation_text.sn1.39:5.3: After giving up this human body,
translation_text.sn1.39:5.4: they swell the hosts of gods.”
